message:
feat: add --json support to spawn/create/add/send commands (ntm-ctt)

Add structured JSON output support to action commands:
- spawn: Returns SpawnResponse with session, panes, agent_counts
- create: Returns CreateResponse with session info and panes
- add: Returns AddResponse with added agent counts and new panes
- send: Uses SendResult struct with delivery status

All commands now support --json flag for machine-readable output:
- Error cases return ErrorResponse with error message
- Success cases return timestamped responses with full details
- Text output suppressed in JSON mode
- Directory auto-created without prompts in JSON mode

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-84t","title":"Implement auto-restart for crashed agents","description":"Automatically restart agents that crash or exit unexpectedly.

## What This Task Does

Add --auto-restart flag to ntm spawn that monitors agents and restarts
them if they crash. This builds on the health check infrastructure.

## Interface

$ ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --auto-restart
Starting agents with auto-restart enabled...
[Agent cc_1 crashed, restarting in 30s...]
[Agent cc_1 restarted successfully]

Configuration:
[resilience]
auto_restart = true
max_restarts = 3
restart_delay_seconds = 30

## Technical Approach

Option 1: Background goroutine
- Spawn launches a monitor goroutine
- Periodically checks health
- Restarts crashed agents
- Exits when session ends

Option 2: Separate daemon
- ntm spawn --auto-restart starts a background process
- Process monitors and restarts
- Survives terminal close

Recommendation: Start with Option 1 (simpler), evolve to Option 2 if needed.

## Restart Logic

1. Detect crash via health check (exit code != 0, process gone)
2. Check restart count for this agent
3. If under max_restarts:
   a. Wait restart_delay_seconds
   b. Get agent type from pane title
   c. Re-run agent command in same pane
   d. Increment restart count
4. If over max_restarts:
   a. Log failure
   b. Send notification (if enabled)
   c. Stop monitoring this agent

## State Tracking

Need to track per-agent:
- Restart count since session start
- Last restart time
- Original agent command

Store in:
- Memory (simple, lost on ntm exit)
- Tmux environment variable (persists)
- File (overkill for now)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --auto-restart flag enables monitoring
- [ ] Crashed agents are detected and restarted
- [ ] Restart delay is configurable
- [ ] Max restart limit is enforced
- [ ] Restart count shown in health output
- [ ] Works with existing spawn command

 {"id":"ntm-8h3","title":"EPIC: Automation, Pipelines & Integration","description":"Advanced automation including agent pipelines, webhooks, and external integrations.

## Problem Statement
NTM is currently manual and reactive:
- Each prompt requires explicit user action
- No way to chain agent outputs as inputs to other agents
- No notifications when agents finish or error
- No integration with external tools (CI, Slack, etc.)
- File content must be manually copied into prompts

## Goals
1. Enable pipeline mode (chain agents sequentially)
2. Support webhook/notification on events
3. Easy file content injection into prompts
4. Token/cost estimation before sending expensive prompts

## Success Criteria
- `ntm pipeline` chains agent stages with output passing
- Config supports on_error, on_complete webhooks
- `ntm send --file=path` injects file content
- `ntm send --estimate` shows token/cost estimate

## Pipeline Mode
```bash
ntm pipeline myproject \
  --stage="cc: Design the authentication module" \
  --stage="cod: Implement the design from previous stage" \
  --stage="gmi: Write tests for the implementation"
```

Each stage:
1. Sends prompt to specified agent type
2. Waits for completion (detect idle state)
3. Captures output
4. Passes output as context to next stage

## Webhook Config
```toml
[notifications]
on_error = "curl -X POST https://slack.webhook/..."
on_complete = "terminal-notifier -message 'Done'"
on_rate_limit = "echo 'Rate limited' >> ~/ntm-alerts.log"
```

## File Context
```bash
ntm send myproject --all \
  --file=src/auth.py \
  --file=src/models.py:10-50 \
  "Refactor these files to use dependency injection"
```

Injects files with formatting:
```
# File: src/auth.py
\`\`\`python
<file contents>
\`\`\`
```

## Cost Estimation
- Count tokens in prompt using tiktoken or similar
- Estimate based on known pricing (Claude, Codex, Gemini)
- Show estimate, ask for confirmation on expensive prompts

## User Value
- Sophisticated multi-stage workflows
- Stay informed without constant monitoring
- Faster prompt construction with file injection
- Budget awareness for API-heavy usage","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:38:05.291885-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:38:05.291885-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-8h3.1","title":"File Context Injection (--file flag)","description":"Add --file flag to ntm send to inject file contents into prompts.

## Use Case
Instead of manually copying file contents, specify files to include:
```bash
ntm send myproject --all \
  --file=src/auth.py \
  --file=src/models.py \
  "Refactor these to use dependency injection"
```

## Prompt Injection Format
Files are prepended to prompt with clear formatting:
```
# File: src/auth.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/auth.py>
\`\`\`

# File: src/models.py
\`\`\`python
<contents of src/models.py>
\`\`\`

---

Refactor these to use dependency injection
```

## Extended Syntax
```bash
--file=path                    # Entire file
--file=path:10-50              # Lines 10-50
--file=path:10-                # Line 10 to end
--file=path:-50                # First 50 lines
--file=path:func:my_function   # Extract function (future)
```

## Implementation
```go
type FileSpec struct {
    Path      string
    StartLine int  // 0 = from beginning
    EndLine   int  // 0 = to end
}

func ParseFileSpec(spec string) (FileSpec, error) {
    // Parse "path:start-end" format
}

func InjectFiles(specs []FileSpec, prompt string) (string, error) {
    var parts []string
    
    for _, spec := range specs {
        content, err := readFileRange(spec)
        if err != nil {
            return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read %s: %w", spec.Path, err)
        }
        
        lang := detectLanguage(spec.Path)
        header := fmt.Sprintf("# File: %s", spec.Path)
        if spec.StartLine > 0 || spec.EndLine > 0 {
            header += fmt.Sprintf(" (lines %d-%d)", spec.StartLine, spec.EndLine)
        }
        
        parts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf("%s\n```%s\n%s\n```", 
            header, lang, content))
    }
    
    parts = append(parts, "---\n\n"+prompt)
    return strings.Join(parts, "\n\n"), nil
}
```

## Language Detection
Map file extensions to markdown language tags:
```go
var langMap = map[string]string{
    ".py":   "python",
    ".go":   "go",
    ".js":   "javascript",
    ".ts":   "typescript",
    ".rs":   "rust",
    ".rb":   "ruby",
    ".java": "java",
    // etc.
}
```

## Integration with Templates
Templates can reference {{file}} and {{files}}:
```markdown
---
name: review
variables:
  file: required
---
Review the following file for issues:

{{file}}

Focus on security and performance.
```

## Flags
| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| -f, --file | File to include (repeatable) |
| --file-format | Format: markdown (default), raw, xml |
| --no-file-header | Omit file path headers |

## Edge Cases
- File not found: Error with clear message
- Binary files: Skip with warning
- Very large files: Warn if > 50KB, --force to override
- Relative vs absolute paths: Resolve relative to cwd

## Files to Modify
- internal/cli/send.go - Add --file flag processing
- internal/prompt/inject.go - File injection logic (new)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.519465-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-8h3.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-8h3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:43:58.520574-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-8js","title":"Implement ntm personas list/show commands","description":"# Implement ntm personas list/show Commands

## Purpose

Allow users to discover and inspect available personas.

## Commands

```bash
# List all personas
ntm personas list
ntm personas list --json

# Show specific persona
ntm personas show architect
ntm personas show architect --json
```

## List Output

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Available Personas                                         │
├──────────────┬────────────┬────────────────────────────────┤
│ Name         │ Agent      │ Description                    │
├──────────────┼────────────┼────────────────────────────────┤
│ architect    │ claude     │ Senior software architect      │
│ implementer  │ claude     │ Fast implementation engineer   │
│ reviewer     │ claude     │ Meticulous code reviewer       │
│ tester       │ codex      │ Test engineer                  │
│ documenter   │ gemini     │ Documentation writer           │
├──────────────┴────────────┴────────────────────────────────┤
│ Source: 3 built-in, 2 user (~/.config/ntm/personas.toml)   │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Show Output

```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Persona: architect                                         │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Agent Type:   claude                                       │
│ Model:        opus                                         │
│ Temperature:  0.7                                          │
│ Source:       built-in                                     │
│ Tags:         design, review                               │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ System Prompt:                                             │
│                                                            │
│ You are a senior software architect. Focus on:             │
│ - System design and high-level structure                   │
│ - Reviewing and guiding implementation                     │
│ - Identifying architectural risks                          │
│ - NOT writing implementation code (delegate that)          │
├────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Context Files:                                             │
│ - ARCHITECTURE.md                                          │
│ - docs/design/*.md                                         │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## JSON Output

```json
{
  "name": "architect",
  "description": "Senior software architect",
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "model": "opus",
  "temperature": 0.7,
  "system_prompt": "...",
  "context_files": ["ARCHITECTURE.md", "docs/design/*.md"],
  "tags": ["design", "review"],
  "source": "built-in"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] ntm personas list shows all personas
- [ ] ntm personas show displays details
- [ ] Source indicator (built-in, user, project)
- [ ] --json output for scripts
- [ ] Filtering by tag or agent type","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteStone","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:49.772792-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T04:39:02.092809-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T04:39:02.092809-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-8js","depends_on_id":"ntm-hzl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:01.048364-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-a8q","title":"Implement --robot-ack for send confirmation","description":"Track and confirm message receipt by agents.

## Rationale
After --robot-send, I need to know:
1. Did the agent receive the message?
2. Did the agent acknowledge understanding?
3. Is the agent actively working on it?

Without this, I'm blind after sending - I have to poll tail output and parse for confirmation patterns.

## Proposal: Ack Tracking
When sending with --robot-send --track, ntm:
1. Watches pane output for acknowledgment patterns
2. Returns structured confirmation

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "sent_at": "ISO8601",
  "confirmations": [
    {
      "pane": "0.1",
      "ack_type": "prompt_returned",  // agent showed ready prompt
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "latency_ms": 234
    },
    {
      "pane": "0.2",
      "ack_type": "echo_detected",    // agent echoed input
      "ack_at": "ISO8601",
      "latency_ms": 156
    }
  ],
  "pending": ["0.3"],  // still waiting
  "failed": []
}
```

## Ack Detection Patterns
- prompt_returned: Agent shows ready prompt after processing
- echo_detected: Input echoed in pane (basic confirmation)
- explicit_ack: Agent responds with understood/acknowledged
- output_started: Agent begins producing output

## Timeout Handling
--timeout=30s - wait up to 30s for acks

 {"id":"ntm-a9nk","title":"Enable UBS dogfooding: run scans on NTM codebase","description":"## Overview
NTM should eat its own dog food by using UBS to scan its own Go codebase, demonstrating the integration and catching real issues.

## Background
Per AGENTS.md: 'ubs <changed-files> before every commit. Exit 0 = safe.'

If NTM integrates UBS, it should use it on itself. This:
- Proves the integration works on real code
- Catches bugs in NTM's Go code
- Provides example output for documentation
- Builds confidence in the tooling

## Implementation

### CI Integration
- Add UBS scan step to any CI/CD pipeline
- Fail PR if critical issues found
- Cache UBS installation for speed

### Developer Workflow  
- Document in README: 'Run `ntm scan .` before commits'
- Or just `ubs .` until ntm scan is implemented
- Add to CONTRIBUTING guidelines (if we had one)

### Initial Scan
- Run full scan on current codebase
- Triage and fix any critical/error findings
- Document false positives for future reference

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Initial full scan completed
- [ ] Critical issues fixed or documented as false positives
- [ ] CI integration (if applicable)
- [ ] Developer workflow documented","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:39:09.927684-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:52:19.449596-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:52:19.449596-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-a9nk","depends_on_id":"ntm-lk66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:39:19.512658-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

Verify configuration affects command behavior correctly.

- Open palette
- Verify commands appear correctly

### Scenario 4: Palette from Markdown
- Create command_palette.md
- Verify commands loaded from markdown

### Scenario 5: Config Precedence
- Set value in both config file and default
- Verify file takes precedence

- Create temp config files per test
- Use environment variables for paths
- Cleanup temp dirs after test","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:17.049961-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:17.049961-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ad3","depends_on_id":"ntm-66c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:41.540817-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-ad3","depends_on_id":"ntm-7lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:42.610247-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-aqqa","title":"Implement pre-commit hook for UBS quality gate","description":"## Overview
Create a pre-commit hook that runs UBS scans and blocks commits containing critical issues, ensuring code quality standards are maintained.

## Background
Pre-commit hooks provide the last line of defense before code enters the repository. Integrating UBS here:
- Catches issues before they're committed
- Provides immediate feedback to developers/agents
- Enforces quality standards consistently

## Implementation Details

### Hook Behavior
1. On commit, run UBS on staged files only (fast path)
2. Parse results and check against configurable thresholds
3. Block commit if thresholds exceeded
4. Output clear, actionable error messages

### Configuration (.ntm.yaml or similar)
```yaml
scan:
  pre_commit:
    enabled: true
    block_on:
      critical: true  # Block on any critical
      error: 5        # Block if >5 errors
      warning: false  # Don't block on warnings
    timeout: 30s
    exclude:
      - "*.test.go"
      - "vendor/**"
```

### Hook Installation
- `ntm hooks install` - Install pre-commit hook
- `ntm hooks uninstall` - Remove hook
- Hook script delegates to `ntm scan --staged --strict`

### Output Format
```
🚫 Commit blocked by NTM quality gate

Found 2 critical issues in staged files:

  src/auth/login.go:45 [critical] SQL injection vulnerability
  src/api/handler.go:123 [critical] Unhandled error return

Run 'ntm scan --staged' for details.
To bypass: git commit --no-verify (not recommended)
```

### Files to Create/Modify
- internal/hooks/precommit.go - Hook logic
- internal/hooks/install.go - Hook installation
- internal/cli/hooks.go - hooks subcommand
- scripts/pre-commit.sh - Actual hook script template

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hook installs correctly in git repo
- [ ] Only staged files are scanned (fast)
- [ ] Configurable blocking thresholds
- [ ] Clear error messages with file:line references
- [ ] Bypass option documented but discouraged","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteStone","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:33:34.385746-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:56:22.357164-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:56:22.357164-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-aqqa","depends_on_id":"ntm-p77j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:34:59.453887-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-aqqa","depends_on_id":"ntm-lk66","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:35:16.639902-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-nxf","title":"Integrate context status into dashboard","description":"# Integrate Context Status into Dashboard

## Purpose

Show context usage in the dashboard view for at-a-glance monitoring.

## Dashboard Enhancement

Add context bar to each pane card:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Panes (4)                                                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● cc_1   opus      working    [████████░░] 62%              │
│ ● cc_2   sonnet    idle       [█████████░] 89% ⚠️           │
│ ● cod_1  gpt4      working    [███░░░░░░░] 31%              │
│ ● gmi_1  pro       idle       [░░░░░░░░░░]  5%              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation

Add context field to pane model in dashboard:
```go
type PaneCard struct {
    // Existing fields...
    ContextUsed int
    ContextMax  int
    ContextPct  float64
}
```

Render context bar:
```go
func (p PaneCard) ContextBar(width int) string {
    filled := int(p.ContextPct * float64(width) / 100)
    empty := width - filled
    
    color := theme.Green
    if p.ContextPct > 80 {
        color = theme.Red
    } else if p.ContextPct > 60 {
        color = theme.Yellow
    }
    
    return color.Render(strings.Repeat("█", filled)) + 
           theme.Dim.Render(strings.Repeat("░", empty))
}
```

## Refresh Strategy

- Context data cached for 60s
- Press 'c' to force context refresh
- Background refresh if dashboard open >60s

## Keyboard Shortcut

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| c | Refresh context data |
| C | Open full context view |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Context bar displayed per pane
- [ ] Color coding (green/yellow/red)
- [ ] Warning icon for >80%
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut for refresh
- [ ] Graceful handling when data unavailable","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:13.013354-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:13.013354-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-nxf","depends_on_id":"ntm-jda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:23.526527-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-oba","title":"Implement --robot-terse for ultra-compact output","description":"Single-line encoded state for extremely token-constrained scenarios.

## Rationale
Sometimes I need just the essentials in minimum tokens. A full snapshot might be 500+ tokens; terse mode gives me the critical state in <50 tokens.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-terse
ntm --robot-snapshot --terse  # terse snapshot
```

## Output Format
```
S:myproject|A:3/3|R:30|B:27|I:3|M:5|!:2
```

Decoded:
- S: Session name
- A: Agents (active/total)
- R: Ready beads
- B: Blocked beads  
- I: In-progress beads
- M: Unread mail
- !: Alert count

## Multi-Session
```
S:proj1|A:2/2|R:15|B:10|I:1|M:2|!:0;S:proj2|A:3/3|R:20|B:5|I:2|M:0|!:1
```

## Use Cases
1. Polling in tight loops
2. Embedding in prompts with strict token limits
3. Quick health checks
4. Dashboard status bars

## Parsing
Provide parsing utilities in robot package:
```go
func ParseTerse(s string) *TerseState
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:39.009743-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:39.009743-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-oba","depends_on_id":"ntm-sim","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:39.818091-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"ntm-oln","title":"Implement --robot-spawn for structured session creation","description":"Create session with preset and return structured result.

## Rationale
Current spawn returns human-readable output. For orchestration, I need structured confirmation of exactly what was created.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-spawn myproject --preset=fullstack
ntm --robot-spawn myproject --cc=2 --cod=1
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "created_at": "ISO8601",
  "preset_used": "fullstack",
  "working_dir": "/path/to/project",
  "agents": [
    {"pane": "0.0", "type": "claude", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 234},
    {"pane": "0.1", "type": "claude", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 198},
    {"pane": "0.2", "type": "codex", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 312}
  ],
  "layout": "tiled",
  "total_startup_ms": 512
}
```

## Ready Detection
- Parse pane output for agent prompt/ready indicator
- Timeout after configurable wait (default 30s)
- Report ready=false with reason if agent didn't start

## Integration
Works with recipe system when implemented. Preset name maps to recipe definition.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:55.090595-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:55.090595-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-ozcj","title":"EPIC: Deep CASS Integration - Cross-Agent Session Search","description":"Transform NTM from a session manager into an intelligent AI agent orchestration platform by deeply integrating CASS (Coding Agent Session Search). This enables agents to learn from past sessions, avoid duplicate work, surface institutional knowledge, and provide operators with actionable insights on agent activity.

## Vision
CASS indexes 10+ AI agent conversation histories into a unified, sub-50ms searchable index. Combined with NTM's session management:
- Before solving: Query CASS for similar issues → avoid re-solving
- While working: Surface relevant context from past sessions  
- After solving: New session auto-indexed for future reference
- For operators: Dashboard showing agent activity, trends, insights

## Architecture
- internal/cass/ - Go client for CASS robot mode
- ntm cass <subcommand> - CLI commands for search, status, insights
- --robot-cass-* flags - Machine-readable output
- TUI widgets - CASS search in dashboard

## Success Metrics
- Agents can query CASS in <100ms
- 30% reduction in duplicate problem-solving
- Operators see unified agent activity view","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:27.867341-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:39:02.250344-05:00"}
 {"id":"ntm-ozcj.1","title":"CASS Infrastructure: Create internal/cass Go client package","description":"Create the foundational Go client package for CASS integration.

## Overview
This package provides a Go interface to CASS robot mode, enabling NTM to query past agent sessions, check health, and retrieve insights.

## Files to Create
- internal/cass/client.go - Main client with exec wrapper
- internal/cass/types.go - Response types (SearchHit, Status, Capabilities, etc.)
- internal/cass/search.go - Search API wrapper
- internal/cass/health.go - Health check integration
- internal/cass/timeline.go - Timeline API wrapper
- internal/cass/client_test.go - Unit tests with mocks

## Key Design Decisions

### Execution Model
Use os/exec to call cass CLI (not HTTP) because:
- CASS is a local tool, not a server
- Robot mode is designed for CLI consumption
- No need for daemon/socket infrastructure

### Error Handling
- CASS exit codes map to Go errors
- Retry on transient errors (timeout, busy)
- Graceful degradation when CASS not installed

### Response Types
Mirror CASS robot mode JSON schemas:
```go
type SearchHit struct {
    SourcePath  string  `json:"source_path"`
    LineNumber  *int    `json:"line_number,omitempty"`
    Agent       string  `json:"agent"`
    Workspace   string  `json:"workspace"`
    Title       string  `json:"title"`
    Score       float64 `json:"score"`
    Snippet     string  `json:"snippet"`
    CreatedAt   *int64  `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
    MatchType   string  `json:"match_type"`
    Content     string  `json:"content,omitempty"`
}

type SearchResponse struct {
    Query        string       `json:"query"`
    Limit        int          `json:"limit"`
    Offset       int          `json:"offset"`
    Count        int          `json:"count"`
    TotalMatches int          `json:"total_matches"`
    Hits         []SearchHit  `json:"hits"`
    Meta         *Meta        `json:"_meta,omitempty"`
    Aggregations *Aggregations `json:"aggregations,omitempty"`
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Client detects if CASS is installed
- [ ] Search returns parsed results
- [ ] Health check works
- [ ] Timeout and retry logic
- [ ] All error codes mapped
- [ ] Tests pass with mock CASS output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:49.800387-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:42:47.427671-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:42:47.427671-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-ozcj.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-ozcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:29:49.800822-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
